title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture
statement_text: For every forest $H$ and every integer $k\geq 1$, there exists $c\geq 1$ such that $\chi(G)\leq c$ for every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$.
context_text: The Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture, labelled Conjecture 1.1 in the paper, asserts that excluding a fixed forest $H$ and a fixed clique $K_{k+1}$ as induced subgraphs gives a $\chi$-bounded graph class. In particular it would imply that every such graph has a stable set of linear size — dramatically better than the $\Omega(|G|^{1/k})$ bound available when only $K_{k+1}$ is excluded. The paper takes this as its central motivation.
paper_contribution: Proves that every $\{H,K_{k+1}\}$-free graph $G$ with $H$ a forest satisfies $\alpha(G)\geq |G|^{1-o(1)}$ (Theorem 1.2/1.3/1.4); proves the full linear stable-set conclusion of the conjecture for all 'multibrooms' $H$; and deduces a fractional-colouring version of the conjecture for all multibrooms.
notes: Paper labels the conjecture as item 1.1; original sources are Gyárfás 1975 [5] and Sumner 1981 [18].
